raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Open-ended 'What do you think?' on a theory paper naturally requires reconstructing the authors' thesis: the selective risk framing, SelectMD's design, and the convergence contribution claim — without understanding these, no meaningful opinion can be rendered.","R1.2":"Logical consistency is a natural load of any 'what do you think?' on a formal methods paper; critically, the artifact contains a visible internal contradiction — the introduction claims an accelerated O(1/T) rate while Theorem 1 states an O(1/sqrt(T)) bound — and the smoothness assumption (ii) conflicts with the paper's stated goal of handling non-smooth losses.","R1.3":"The paper pairs asymptotic convergence guarantees with a concrete empirical claim (30% fewer epochs on CIFAR-10 at coverage 0.7); evaluating whether these mutually support the central contribution is naturally in scope for a general impression.","R1.4":"The paper explicitly asserts priority ('first mirror-descent procedure to deliver accelerated convergence on non-smooth selective losses'); a 'what do you think?' on a research contribution includes assessing whether this novelty claim holds against the selective-answering and mirror-descent literatures."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is your primary interest in verifying the technical and mathematical correctness of the claims (convergence proof, internal consistency of assumptions), rather than in assessing the novelty and significance of the contribution relative to prior work in selective prediction and mirror descent?"}

rationale.R1.1: Open-ended 'What do you think?' on a theory paper naturally requires reconstructing the authors' thesis: the selective risk framing, SelectMD's design, and the convergence contribution claim — without understanding these, no meaningful opinion can be rendered.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is a natural load of any 'what do you think?' on a formal methods paper; critically, the artifact contains a visible internal contradiction — the introduction claims an accelerated O(1/T) rate while Theorem 1 states an O(1/sqrt(T)) bound — and the smoothness assumption (ii) conflicts with the paper's stated goal of handling non-smooth losses.
rationale.R1.3: The paper pairs asymptotic convergence guarantees with a concrete empirical claim (30% fewer epochs on CIFAR-10 at coverage 0.7); evaluating whether these mutually support the central contribution is naturally in scope for a general impression.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly asserts priority ('first mirror-descent procedure to deliver accelerated convergence on non-smooth selective losses'); a 'what do you think?' on a research contribution includes assessing whether this novelty claim holds against the selective-answering and mirror-descent literatures.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary interest in verifying the technical and mathematical correctness of the claims (convergence proof, internal consistency of assumptions), rather than in assessing the novelty and significance of the contribution relative to prior work in selective prediction and mirror descent?
